Home.title: Operate nodes, servers, and access in one control surface.
Home.subtitle: Vestri keeps your infrastructure organized with clear permissions, deployment visibility, and secure user flows.

cta.preview: Sign in to open your dashboard and manage nodes, templates, and active game servers.

errors.invalidSession: Your session has expired or is invalid. Please log in again.

hero.title: Set up and run your game server platform with confidence.
hero.subtitle: This guide is intentionally beginner-friendly and uses concrete copy-paste commands for setup, operations, and security.

chapters.title: Chapters
chapters.nodeOnboardingDescription: Set up worker defaults, trust chain, and node credentials first.
chapters.serverLifecycleDescription: Create, run, and update servers using a repeatable step-by-step flow.
chapters.accessSecurityDescription: Grant clear permissions and protect critical actions with step-up checks.
chapters.configReferenceDescription: Understand all environment variables and settings for the worker and stack.

sslCaTrust.title: SSL/TLS CA Trust (Worker -> Backend)
sslCaTrust.description: If worker TLS is enabled, add each worker CA certificate into the backend trust volume before creating the node in Vestri.

prerequisites.title: Before You Start
prerequisites.description: Set this baseline once, then follow the rest of the guide in order for a smoother rollout.

items.1: A Vestri account with access to your workspace
items.2: A reachable worker endpoint (host/port) plus the worker API key
items.3: At least one server template with clear version defaults

prerequisites.quickChecksDescription: If one of these commands fails, fix that first and continue with the guide afterwards.

configReference.title: Configuration Reference
configReference.description: Complete reference of environment variables and worker settings options currently used by the stack.

environment.title: Environment variables
environment.description: Frontend, backend, migration, and bundled compose infrastructure variables.

GO_API_URL.description: Backend base URL used by rewrites, proxy middleware, and API proxy routes.

APP_BASE_URL.description: Base URL for callbacks and generated links.

REDIS_URL.description: Redis connection for sessions and rate limits.

LOG_MAX_SIZE_MB.description: Max log file size before rotation.

WORKER_TLS_CA_CERT_FILE.description: Additional trusted worker CA certificate file.

WORKER_TLS_CA_CERT_DIR.description: Directory with trusted worker CA certs.

NODE_API_KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY.description: Encryption key for stored worker node API keys.

TRUSTED_PROXIES.description: Comma-separated list of trusted proxy IPs/CIDRs.

AUTO_MIGRATE.description: Auto-apply SQL migrations during backend startup.

POSTGRES_USER.description: PostgreSQL user for the bundled stack.

POSTGRES_PASSWORD.description: PostgreSQL password for the bundled stack.

POSTGRES_DB.description: PostgreSQL database name for the bundled stack.

workerSettings.title: Worker settings.json options
workerSettings.description: All available keys from /etc/vestri/settings.json with current defaults and runtime behavior.

tls_auto_generate.description: Auto-generate missing TLS CA/server cert assets.

tls_sans.description: Extra SAN entries for generated server cert.

worker_name.description: Optional worker name included in cert SAN generation.

fs_base_path.description: Safe base path for all worker filesystem operations.

replay_window_seconds.description: Allowed request timestamp skew for signed API requests.

max_archive_request_bytes.description: Max request body for archive endpoints.

max_inline_write_bytes.description: Max request body for inline file writes.

max_unzip_bytes.description: Max extracted size for unzip operations.

require_tls.description: Reject non-TLS requests unless trusted proxy headers indicate HTTPS.

trust_proxy_headers.description: Trust forwarded proxy headers for TLS/IP handling.

configReference.parsingHint: Backend bool env parsing accepts: 1, true, yes. List env values are comma-separated.

bootstrap.title: Config and trust bootstrap
bootstrap.description: Before creating your first node in the UI, prepare worker files and trust chain with these explicit steps.

steps.1: Start the worker once so `settings.json`, certificates, and API key are generated.
steps.3: If TLS is enabled, copy `/etc/vestri/certs/ca.crt` into backend trust.
steps.4: For node creation in UI, keep host/base URL and API key from `/etc/vestri/api.key` ready.

rules.1: If TLS is enabled on the worker, copy CA cert from worker to backend trust (default: /etc/vestri/certs/ca.crt -> ./certs/worker-cas/node-name.crt).
rules.2: If TLS is enabled, use https in node host/base URL (default worker URL style: https://worker-host:8031).
rules.3: Only use http:// if TLS is intentionally disabled on the worker.
rules.4: When adding the node in UI, use API key from the worker key file (default: /etc/vestri/api.key).

bootstrap.commandsHint: Only adjust the backend target path if your Compose/Portainer setup uses a different location.
bootstrap.screenshotLabel: Settings, API key, and CA trust placement

systemd.title: Start services with systemd
systemd.description: A beginner-safe production pattern: frontend/backend/db via Compose or Portainer, worker as a dedicated systemd service.

steps.1: Deploy frontend/backend/postgres/redis via Docker Compose or Portainer.
steps.2: Create a dedicated Linux user `vestri` for the worker process.
steps.4: Reload systemd, enable the service, and verify status.

systemd.composeHint: Only adjust the project path (`/opt/vestri`) if your compose project lives elsewhere.
systemd.userSetupHint: You can run these commands as-is. If the user already exists, it will not be created again.

connectNode.title: Connect Your First Node
connectNode.description: Nodes are the execution layer. You need at least one healthy worker node before creating servers.

steps.2: Enter host/base URL (for example `https://worker-host:8031`) and API key from `/etc/vestri/api.key`.
steps.3: Click `Save` and immediately run the health check.
steps.4: If status is not healthy, verify host, port, TLS scheme (`http/https`), and API key.

createServer.title: Create and Provision a Server
createServer.description: Always create servers from templates so versions, paths, and startup values stay predictable.

steps.1: Open `Dashboard` and select the target node at the top.
steps.2: Click `Create server`, choose a template, and fill identifier + version fields.
steps.3: Confirm required agreements and start provisioning.
steps.4: Open the new server workspace and verify status, startup, and first logs.

operateServer.title: Operate During Runtime
operateServer.description: Use the server workspace as your single place for start/stop, logs, console, and config changes.

steps.1: Control the server with `Start`, `Stop`, and `Refresh status` first.
steps.2: Open logs and verify startup without errors before making changes.
steps.3: Edit files in small steps through file browser/editor (avoid big one-shot changes).
steps.4: After each change, restart and re-check logs + console output.

teamAndAccess.title: Manage Team Access Safely
teamAndAccess.description: Use least-privilege roles and keep sensitive actions behind step-up verification.

steps.1: Assign only the required role per server (`viewer`, `operator`, `admin`).
steps.2: Send invites only when needed and remove stale access regularly.
steps.3: Use step-up checks for critical actions (delete, access changes, passkeys).
steps.4: Review active sessions frequently and rotate credentials on suspicion.

updateRoutine.title: Build a Clean Update Routine
updateRoutine.description: Run updates as a planned, repeatable process so changes stay controlled in production.

steps.1: Check which server images are outdated and prioritize critical workloads.
steps.2: Repull images only during planned maintenance windows.
steps.3: Restart affected servers and validate health, logs, and reachability.
steps.4: Document version, date, and result for every update per environment.

nextSteps.title: Next Steps
nextSteps.description: Start with one node and one template, then expand to a repeatable deployment workflow.
